title: AGENTS.md
text: High-level wrapper reads CSV files disk Handles file /O, logging, orchestrates checks Returns structured results pass/fail status Core screening logic operating data frames Used internally screen_csv() Called directly data already memory Validates file naming conventions Subset full screening pipeline

title: AGENTS.md
text: Checks organized stages, executed sequentially. check fails stage, screening stops returns failure stage. Examples : Filename – Naming conventions special characters Precheck columns – Required columns, column count matching Precheck meta – Metadata structure types Check meta – Metadata content validation (warnings allowed) Precheck time – Time period validity Check API – API-specific constraints (warnings , doesn’t block) check function follows naming pattern: - precheck_* – Early validation blocks failure - check_* – Content validation can produce warnings

title: AGENTS.md
text: check precheck functions follow consistent naming patterns defined _pkgdown.yml. general structure : Common abbreviations used brevity: - col – column - meta – metadata - fil_grp – filter group - dupe – duplicate - ind – indicator - dp – decimal places - geog – geography - ob – observation - loc – location Examples abbreviated function names: - check_meta_dupe_label() – checks duplicate indicator labels - check_meta_fil_grp() – validates filter group structure - check_meta_fil_grp_is_fil() – verifies filter group values filters - check_api_char_col_name() – validates column name character limits API
code: check_<area>_<what>()      # Examples: check_meta_label, check_filter_defaults precheck_<area>_<what>()   # Examples: precheck_col_req_data, precheck_geog_level

title: AGENTS.md
text: checks return consistent data frame columns: - check: Name check function - result: “PASS”, “FAIL”, “WARNING” - message: Human-readable feedback - guidance_url: Optional link documentation - stage: screening stage check belongs screen_csv() screen_dfs() functions return list:
code: list(   results_table = data.frame(...),     # All check results   overall_stage = "Precheck columns",  # Where screening stopped   passed = TRUE/FALSE,                 # Did all required checks pass?   api_suitable = TRUE/FALSE            # Can be published via API? )

title: AGENTS.md
text: Main screening: - screen_csv.R – Entry point file-based screening - screen_dfs.R – Core logic orchestrating checks - screen_filenames.R – File naming validation screen_dfs Architecture: screen_dfs() function executes checks stages using run_and_log_check() helper (defined utils.R). helper: - Accumulates results across stages - Adds stage metadata check result - Logs results JSON (log_key provided) - Returns early FAIL (stops checks) Code structure: uses shorthand variables vb (verbose) soe (stop_on_error) throughout reduce function call verbosity improve readability. Individual check functions (~39 total): - Organized check type: precheck_*.R check_*.R - file contains one public function roxygen2 documentation - Checks composed simple, focused validation logic - Must follow consistent argument order: data/meta inputs, verbose = FALSE, stop_on_error = FALSE, function-specific parameters Utilities (R/utils.R): - test_output() – Standardizes check result data frames - null_filename() – Handles filename display messages - validate_arg_*() – Argument validation helpers - write_json_log() – Logging infrastructure API usage - run_and_log_check() – Helper handles stage management, logging, early-return logic - get_check_name() – Automatically extracts check name calling function (reduces hardcoding) - get_filters(meta, include_filter_groups = FALSE) – Returns character vector filter column names meta; set include_filter_groups = TRUE also include filter_grouping_column values - get_filter_groups(meta) – Returns non-NA, non-blank filter_grouping_column values meta - get_cols_meta(meta, ...) – Extracts column names meta type/criteria - get_geo_code_cols() / get_geo_name_cols() – Returns geography code/name column name vectors - remove_nas_blanks(vector) – Removes NAs empty strings vector - render_url(slug, domain) – Constructs guidance URLs test_output(guidance_url = ...) Always read R/utils.R writing filtering, extraction, transformation logic — many common operations already helpers. Reference data (R/reference_values.R, R/example_datasets.R): - Exports acceptable values, time identifiers, filter groups - Example data documentation testing

title: AGENTS.md
text: Pre-computed RDA files loaded package startup: - acceptable_time_ids.rda – Valid time periods (e.g., “2023/24”, “Academic Year”) - acceptable_indicator_units.rda – Valid indicator units - data_dictionary.rda – Official EES column specifications - api_char_limits.rda – Character limits API publishing - example_*.rda – Minimal example datasets documentation - ees_robot_test_data.rda – Large pre-computed test data

title: AGENTS.md
text: precheck_*() check_*() functions must follow consistent argument order: data /meta (data inputs) verbose = FALSE stop_on_error = FALSE additional function-specific parameters (defaults) allows screen_dfs() call checks positional arguments common parameters (e.g. check_foo(data, meta, vb, soe)). Function-specific parameters must always passed name. adding new validation: Create R file – R/check_my_validation.R : Add screening pipeline – Call screen_dfs.R appropriate stage Create test – tests/testthat/test-check_my_validation.R passing/failing cases Document – roxygen2 comments @export, examples, @param descriptions Update NAMESPACE – Run devtools::document()
code: #' @export check_my_validation <- function(data, meta, verbose = FALSE, stop_on_error = FALSE) {   check_name <- get_check_name()  # never hardcode the name string   # validation logic   test_output(     check_name,     "PASS",  # or "FAIL"/"WARNING"     "message...",     verbose = verbose,     stop_on_error = stop_on_error   ) }

code: files <- eesyscreener::generate_test_dfs(   years = 2013:2015,    pcon_names = "Sheffield Central",    pcon_codes = "E14000919",    num_filters = 2,    num_indicators = 3 )  # Data and metadata are returned in a list, to extract: df <- files$data df_meta <- files$meta # Get a data frame of all Parliamentary Constituencies in England pcons <- dfeR::fetch_pcons(countries = "England")  beefy <- eesyscreener::generate_test_dfs(   years = c(1980:2025),   pcon_codes = pcons$pcon_code,   pcon_names = pcons$pcon_name,   num_filters = 3,   num_indicators = 45,   verbose = TRUE )  # Then to create CSVs, use duckplyr as it's a lot faster than base R # roughly ~20 seconds compared to ~6 minutes duckplyr::compute_csv(beefy$data, "beefy_data.csv") write.csv(beefy$meta, "beefy_data.meta.csv", row.names = FALSE)
